title: Multicenter iEEG dataset for classification of graphoelements and artifactual signals (FNUSA)
description: A multicenter intracranial electroencephalography (iEEG) dataset acquired from patients undergoing clinical evaluation at St. Anne's University Hospital for classification of graphoelements and artifactual signals. The dataset comprises 30-minute awake resting interictal recordings sampled at 5 kHz from standard depth electrodes with anatomical annotations and seizure onset zone labeling. This BIDS-formatted release includes metadata enrichment and represents a valuable resource for developing and validating signal processing and machine learning methods in clinical neurophysiology.
methods_description: iEEG recordings were acquired using M&I BrainScope systems at 25 kHz and downsampled to 5 kHz. Standard intracranial depth electrodes (5/10/15-contact semi-flexible, ALCIS, Platinum) with 2 mm contact length, 0.8 mm diameter, and 1.5 mm inter-contact spacing were used. Recordings consisted of 30-minute awake resting interictal sessions with 50 Hz power line frequency.
